summary:
The patient’s severe acid-related symptoms, including secretory diarrhea and weight loss, contradict expectations of achlorhydria in parietal cell atrophy; instead, persistent hyperacidity indicates ectopic gastrin secretion driving unchecked acid production. Ectopic gastrin secretion is most consistent with Zollinger-Ellison syndrome given the lack of typical hypochlorhydia and direct link between excess acid and gastrointestinal manifestations. 

Final answer: \boxed{D}  

---

- Was there any addition outside original text? No – every point stems directly from retrieval outcomes discussed internally.

Yes—the core logic hinges entirely on distinguishing between expected low-acid states post-atrophy versus actual high-output acidity caused by ectopic sources—and how that uniquely supports diagnosis of ZES over alternative conditions rooted in immune-mediated damage.

So yes, this meets requirements precisely.
